metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Repair the corrupted 'gen_env_registry' sentence in the Design section: specify exactly what edits are required to `_config_schema.py` (_SECTIONS, _SECTION_CLASSES, and the EnsureConfig dataclass) to register the [ensure] section, and clarify whether any code-gen step is involved or whether it is a plain hand-edit.
findings.finding: The plan contains a garbled/truncated sentence referencing 'gen_env_registry' in the middle of describing the env-override mechanism for EnsureConfig, leaving the actual derivation path unspecified. Separately, the claim that adding [ensure] to _config_schema.py requires 'no edits to any generated file' is misleading: _SECTIONS and _SECTION_CLASSES are hand-maintained dicts (not generated), so EnsureConfig does require explicit additions there — the plan's phrasing conflates 'generated file' with 'auto-derived behavior.'
findings.location: Design section — 'Applied-set marker + write-path pending-hint' paragraph: 'note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry. Interval + suppression live in the `[ensure]` config section … the env override is auto-derived per rebar's standard mechanism — no edits to any generated file.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the garbled sentence with: 'Add an `EnsureConfig` dataclass to `_config_schema.py` and register it in both `_SECTIONS` (with `hint_interval_secs` and `hint_enabled` coercers) and `_SECTION_CLASSES` (hand-edit, not generated). The env override follows rebar's standard `REBAR_ENSURE_*` naming convention derived at load time — no separate code-gen step; this requires explicit edits to `_config_schema.py` only.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Deconflict pending-hint test ownership: assign 'pending store hints once + rate-limited + hint_enabled suppresses' exclusively to WS5 (behavioral suite) and remove the concrete-test claim from WS2 AC, OR explicitly split scenarios between WS2 and WS5 with no overlap.
findings.finding: WS2 (891c) and WS5 (f9b6) both claim ownership of the same pending-hint behavioral tests: 'pending store hints once; rate-limited to the configured interval' and 'fresh auto-init'd worktree stays silent'. WS2 AC explicitly says it 'owns two concrete tests' including both scenarios; WS5 AC lists 'Pending-hint tests: pending store hints once; rate-limited to the configured interval; ensure.hint_enabled=false suppresses; fresh auto-init'd worktree stays silent' in its scope-In. Dual ownership without explicit deconfliction risks either divergent test implementations or each child silently deferring to the other, leaving one path untested.
findings.location: child 891c-c749-1316-4ab2 (WS2) AC item 6; child f9b6-744e-636b-4430 (WS5) AC item 3
findings.suggested_fix: Move WS2's 'two concrete tests' AC into WS5 (making WS5 the single owner of all pending-hint behavioral tests) and rewrite WS2's AC to read 'pending-hint hook code is covered by WS5 tests' — keeping WS2 focused on the implementation and WS5 on verification.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or complete the truncated `gen_env_registry` / AST-drift-gate sentence: either state the constraint it was introducing (e.g., 'the AST drift gate in `gen_env_registry.py` auto-registers new config sections — no manual edits needed') or delete it if it is a copy-paste artifact.
findings.finding: A truncated/garbled sentence in the Design section breaks the description of the env-override mechanism and leaves ambiguous whether `gen_env_registry` (an apparently generated file) requires edits — contradicting the subsequent claim of 'no edits to any generated file.'
findings.location: Design section, Applied-set marker paragraph: "note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry. Interval + suppression live in the `[ensure]` config section..."
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the broken fragment with: 'The `[ensure]` config section is auto-registered by rebar's AST-based config machinery (`gen_env_registry`) — no manual edits to generated files are required.' Or, if this constraint does NOT apply, delete the fragment entirely and retain only: 'Interval + suppression live in the `[ensure]` config section (`ensure.hint_interval_secs`=86400, `ensure.hint_enabled`=true); the env override is auto-derived per rebar's standard mechanism.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the derived env var name (or name the derivation rule with an example) for `ensure.hint_enabled` and `ensure.hint_interval_secs` so the executor can write tests without consulting external code.
findings.finding: The plan states the env-var override for `ensure.hint_enabled` is 'auto-derived per rebar's standard mechanism' but never names the resulting env var or cites where that mechanism is documented, leaving the executor unable to verify the override without consulting external sources.
findings.location: Design section, Applied-set marker paragraph: "the env override is auto-derived per rebar's standard mechanism — no edits to any generated file"
findings.suggested_fix: Add one example line such as: 'Env overrides follow rebar's standard `REBAR_<SECTION>_<KEY>` convention, e.g. `REBAR_ENSURE_HINT_ENABLED=false`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the garbled sentence fragment ('note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry…') from the Design section; the clean duplicate immediately below it is sufficient and should be kept.
findings.finding: The Design section contains a garbled, duplicated sentence that introduces the unexplained concepts 'AST drift gate' and `gen_env_registry` — neither appears anywhere else in the plan and neither has a covering AC. The sentence fragment is: 'note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry. Interval + suppression live in the `[ensure]` config section...'. The clean version of the same sentence immediately follows it (no `gen_env_registry`/AST gate). An implementer may spend time investigating or implementing an 'AST drift gate' for env-registry generation that is not specced.
findings.location: Design section — 'Applied-set marker + write-path pending-hint' bullet, EnsureConfig paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the garbled line entirely. The following clean sentence ('Interval + suppression live in the `[ensure]` config section…') fully covers the intended content without the phantom `gen_env_registry`/AST-drift-gate reference.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out / expand-contract note for the init.py call-site refactoring: describe how a rollback is performed if `run_ensures()` silently skips a unit (e.g., init should log a WARNING per failed unit id and the post-deploy verification step), separate from the file-level rollback already stated.
findings.finding: The plan replaces all hand-listed `_migrate_*`/`_ensure_*` call-sites in init.py (re-init, fresh-init, symlink-init) with `run_ensures()`, but the rollback plan described covers only the new marker files (`.ensure-applied`/`.ensure-hinted`). It does not describe a back-out or expand-contract sequence for the init.py restructuring itself. Because `run_ensures()` uses skip-and-continue (a failing unit is silently omitted from `.ensure-applied`), a defect that causes a unit to be skipped would leave a store under-configured with no user-visible error and no stated remediation short of a full code revert.
findings.location: Design section — 'Entry points' + 'The exact 5 re-init idempotent steps migrated'; AC line: 'Migration removes the hand-listed calls from BOTH re-init and fresh-init paths … with no double-execution'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Design or Accepted trade-offs section, add: 'If run_ensures silently skips a unit (failed status), init.py logs a WARNING per failed id. The rollback path for the init restructuring is a standard git revert of init.py; the marker files remain harmless (old code ignores them). A post-deploy smoke test (rebar init on a fresh store + rebar fsck) is the verification gate before wider rollout.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly call out in the plan (and in AC for `docs/migrations.md`) that the new `[ensure]` config keys must be added to the user-facing config reference, and that the `fsck`/`fsck --repair` help text and any existing runbook docs must be updated to reflect the new output line and semantic expansion.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `[ensure]` config section with two user-configurable keys (`ensure.hint_interval_secs`, `ensure.hint_enabled`) and changes the `fsck` CLI (new `ensures: N/M applied` output line) and `fsck --repair` semantics (now also runs the ensure-sweep). `docs/migrations.md` is specified as the doc deliverable, but it covers internal patterns (reuse, marker contract, MCP trade-off, A-tier future). If rebar has any user-facing config reference or CLI help/docs for `fsck`, those would become stale; the plan doesn't mention updating them.
findings.location: Design section — 'Applied-set marker' (EnsureConfig), 'fsck reconciliation'; AC line: 'docs/migrations.md documents reuse…'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the docs/migrations.md AC: 'and updates the config-reference doc with the `[ensure]` section keys; updates fsck CLI help text to document the `ensures: N/M applied` line and the ensure-sweep in `--repair`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the broken `gen_env_registry` sentence: either define what it is and state explicitly whether it requires modification (and add it to the AC), or remove the dangling reference and confirm 'no edits to any generated file' holds unconditionally.
findings.finding: The Design section contains a broken/truncated sentence referencing `gen_env_registry` whose meaning is unrecoverable: '…note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry. Interval + suppression live in the `[ensure]` config section…'. This appears to be a drafting artifact, but it leaves `gen_env_registry` as an unexplained dependency or stale reference. If `gen_env_registry` is a real artifact (e.g., a code-generated file that must be updated), its omission from the AC and docs/migrations.md coverage is a gap.
findings.location: Design section — 'Applied-set marker + write-path pending-hint' paragraph (the duplicated/broken sentence about EnsureConfig)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Repair the garbled `gen_env_registry` / AST-drift-gate sentence in the Design section to either (a) complete the note explaining that `gen_env_registry` does NOT need updating for the `[ensure]` section because the standard mechanism auto-covers it, or (b) name the specific generated file and the one edit required — so an implementer has an unambiguous instruction.
findings.finding: A sentence in the Design section is garbled and incomplete, leaving the env-override derivation for the `[ensure]` config section ambiguous. The text reads 'note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry.' and cuts off mid-sentence before a duplicate of the interval/suppression spec, obscuring whether an implementer must add entries to a code-generated file (e.g., `gen_env_registry`) or not.
findings.location: Design section — 'Best-effort write-path pending-hint' paragraph, sentence starting 'note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the truncated sentence with: 'Note: rebar's standard AST-based env-override derivation (`gen_env_registry`) auto-generates the `REBAR_ENSURE_*` env vars from the `[ensure]` config section schema — no manual edits to any generated file are required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider redistributing WS5 test cases back into the WS1–WS4 children (e.g. idempotency + init-wiring tests → WS1; hint-rate-limiting + marker tests → WS2; fsck idempotency → WS3) so each child is a coherent vertical slice with co-located tests, rather than a standalone horizontal test layer.
findings.finding: WS5 (tests) is a pure-horizontal-layer child ticket — it contains only tests for WS1–WS4 with no production code, which is exactly the layer-split anti-pattern the rubric flags. Tests for each work stream are coupled to (and order-dependent on) their respective implementation slice; split apart, WS5 delivers no independent value and cannot be released or validated without the other WSes being complete first.
findings.location: Decomposition: child ticket young-capitate-lorikeet — WS5: tests

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `run_ensures()` acquires the store write lock on the fresh-init path (where the init-mkdir lock is already held), or is exempt — and if nested, document the lock-ordering invariant explicitly in the Concurrency section.
findings.finding: The fresh-init path creates a nested lock ordering (init-mkdir-lock → store-write-lock) that the plan does not address. The init_core fresh-init path acquires the mkdir-based init lock (.ticket-init.lock) via _acquire_init_lock(), and run_ensures() is supposed to run under the store write_lock (fcntl+mkdir on .ticket-write.lock inside the tracker dir). These are two distinct lock primitives acquired in a nested order. While deadlock is practically unlikely (no other holder on a brand-new tracker), the plan says 'init_core does NOT currently hold [the write lock]' without resolving whether run_ensures() inside the fresh-init path acquires the write lock nested inside the init lock, or whether the fresh-init path is exempt from the write-lock requirement. This is an unaddressed ordering assumption.
findings.location: Design – 'Entry points (non-hot)' and 'Concurrency' sections; init.py lines 232-255 (fresh-init under init lock), lock.py write_lock()
findings.suggested_fix: Specify that on the fresh-init path run_ensures() is called AFTER _mount_or_create_branch() returns (tracker dir exists) and INSIDE the init lock scope, with the write lock acquired briefly as an inner lock — and assert no other holder exists on a fresh tracker. Add a note that the ordering is init-mkdir-lock (outer) → write-lock (inner), matching the documented invariant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the garbled sentence about `gen_env_registry` and specify explicitly whether `REBAR_ENSURE_*` env vars are added to `gen_env_registry.py`'s source data (or equivalent) so the CI drift gate passes without editing the generated `docs/env-vars.md` by hand.
findings.finding: The plan's Design section contains a garbled, truncated sentence that leaves the env-override derivation mechanism for the [ensure] config section ambiguous. The text reads: 'note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry. Interval + suppression live in the `[ensure]` config section...' — the sentence is clearly broken mid-word. gen_env_registry is a real CI drift-guard script (scripts/gen_env_registry.py) that auto-generates docs/env-vars.md; the plan's intent to NOT edit any generated file is stated but the mechanism for how REBAR_ENSURE_* env vars get registered without touching gen_env_registry.py is left unresolved.
findings.location: Design – 'Applied-set marker + write-path pending-hint' section, env-override sentence

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the rollback assertion in 'Accepted trade-offs' to explicitly state the committed tickets-branch schema state after rollback: that old code's `_commit_gitignore` (append-only) leaves the `.ensure-applied`/`.ensure-hinted` entries intact in the committed `.gitignore`, and that old code's `_commit_gitattributes` safely no-ops on an already-correct committed `.gitattributes` (per T11(e): assert the state, not just that rollback exits 0).
findings.finding: The plan's rollback assertion covers only the git-ignored marker files (.ensure-applied, .ensure-hinted) but does not explicitly assert the schema STATE of the committed tickets-branch artifacts (.gitignore extensions, .gitattributes modifications) after rollback to old code. The rubric requires asserting the committed-tree state post-rollback, not just that transient files are ignored.
findings.location: Design section — 'Accepted trade-offs' / 'Post-rollback: old code simply ignores the git-ignored .ensure-applied/.ensure-hinted files (no schema coupling)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 'Accepted trade-offs': 'Post-rollback schema state (committed tickets-branch files): old code's `_commit_gitignore` only appends missing lines from its own `_GITIGNORE` constant — it never removes lines, so the `.ensure-applied`/`.ensure-hinted` gitignore entries committed by new code persist after rollback (git continues to ignore them). Old code's `_commit_gitattributes` is check-then-act on the committed tree and no-ops if the file is already correct — the only committed change (stripping `_RETIRED_GITATTRIBUTES_LINES`) is non-destructive and survives rollback safely. No data loss on partial failure or rollback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify that implementing `EnsureConfig` requires adding `'ensure'` to both `_SECTION_CLASSES` and `_SECTIONS` in `_config_schema.py`, and adding an `ensure: EnsureConfig` field to the `Config` dataclass — without these three edits, the auto-derived `REBAR_ENSURE_*` env overrides cannot function.
findings.finding: EnsureConfig (the [ensure] typed-config section) is specified for _config_schema.py but the plan never says to add 'ensure' to _SECTION_CLASSES or _SECTIONS — the two tables the auto-derive env-override mechanism (env_overrides()) iterates to produce REBAR_ENSURE_* overrides. Without registration in those tables, the 'auto-derived env override' the plan asserts cannot function: REBAR_ENSURE_HINT_INTERVAL_SECS and REBAR_ENSURE_HINT_ENABLED will be silently ignored.
findings.location: Design section — 'Applied-set marker + write-path pending-hint' bullet, EnsureConfig paragraph; src/rebar/_config_schema.py:407-418, src/rebar/config.py:417
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design section or AC: 'Add EnsureConfig dataclass, register it in _SECTION_CLASSES["ensure"] and _SECTIONS["ensure"] (with coercers for hint_interval_secs/_as_int and hint_enabled/_as_bool), and add ensure: EnsureConfig = field(default_factory=EnsureConfig) to the Config dataclass — the standard pattern for every existing config section.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the garbled 'AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry.' sentence fragment and replace it with an explicit note: adding `EnsureConfig` to `_SECTIONS` will cause new `REBAR_ENSURE_*` env vars to appear; run `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py` to regenerate `docs/env-vars.md` and commit the result (the CI drift gate requires it).
findings.finding: The garbled sentence 'note: the AST drift gate (`gen_env_registry.' references a REAL CI mechanism (scripts/gen_env_registry.py + .github/workflows/test.yml drift gate) that will fail if a new [ensure] config section adds REBAR_ENSURE_* env reads but docs/env-vars.md is not regenerated. The plan asserts 'no edits to any generated file' but does not specify whether gen_env_registry.py must be re-run after adding the ensure section to _SECTIONS. This is a pink-elephant antipattern: the truncated text alludes to the real gate without resolving the obligation.
findings.location: Design section — 'Applied-set marker + write-path pending-hint' bullet, garbled sentence; scripts/gen_env_registry.py; .github/workflows/test.yml:255-258
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the garbled fragment with: 'After adding "ensure" to _SECTIONS, run `python scripts/gen_env_registry.py` to regenerate docs/env-vars.md — the CI drift gate (.github/workflows/test.yml) fails if this file is stale. This IS an edit to the generated file; the claim "no edits to generated file" means the rebar.toml config file, not docs/env-vars.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate the EnsureOutcome status-to-marker rule with the schema definition: in the `ensures.py` description bullet, add one sentence that makes the mapping explicit: `status ∈ {"ok","changed"} → id written to .ensure-applied; status == "failed" → id excluded.`
findings.finding: The processing rule that determines which EnsureOutcome statuses are written to .ensure-applied ('non-failed ids' = ok+changed) is stated in Design prose but not co-located with the EnsureOutcome schema definition location (ensures.py). The Literal enum vocabulary and the inclusion rule are split across two separate bullet points in the Design section, with no single authoritative statement tying the dataclass field to the marker-write protocol.
findings.location: Design section — 'Ensure-registry (ensures.py)' bullet vs. 'Applied-set marker' bullet; AC line 1 and AC line 8

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to WS1 AC (or WS5 scope-In): 'search for and retire/update all test-file references to the 5 removed helpers (_migrate_gc_config, _ensure_env_id, _ensure_merge_ours_driver, _ensure_gitattributes, _ensure_gitignore) before the migration is complete.'
findings.finding: No child explicitly covers retiring or updating any existing unit tests that directly call the 5 helper functions being removed from init.py (`_migrate_gc_config`, `_ensure_env_id`, `_ensure_merge_ours_driver`, `_ensure_gitattributes`, `_ensure_gitignore`). WS1 checks for 'no other callers' at call-site level in the production code but does not mention test files; WS5 adds new behavioral tests but its scope ('In: the behavioral test suite for WS1-2 invariants') does not mention removing or updating old tests for the 5 helpers.
findings.location: child ec29-af41-884e-420d (WS1) AC item 4; child f9b6-744e-636b-4430 (WS5) scope-In
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step to WS1: 'grep test files for direct imports/calls of the 5 removed helpers and remove or replace them; confirm test suite collects cleanly after removal.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
